title: Max Coins from Bursting Balloons
description:
You are given n balloons, each balloon has a number. After bursting balloon i you gain nums[left]*nums[i]*nums[right]. The left and right neighbors are the closest unburst balloons. Return the maximum coins you can collect by bursting in optimal order.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 300
- 1 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 100
explanation:
Intuition
Interval DP: pad nums with 1 at both ends. Let dp[l][r] be max coins from open interval (l,r). Choose a last balloon k in (l,r) to burst; gain nums[l]*nums[k]*nums[r] plus dp[l][k]+dp[k][r].

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Pad nums with 1 at both ends
2. Main Algorithm: For len from 2..n+1, compute dp on intervals and try all k between
3. Return Result: Return dp[0][n+1]

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n^3)
- Space Complexity: O(n^2)

Key Insights
- Choosing last burst avoids dependency cycles
- Padding simplifies boundaries

Common Pitfalls
- Wrongly choosing first burst complicates state
- Indices are over padded array of length n+2

testCases.explanation: Burst 5 then 1 or vice versa with padding
When max_coins(nums=[7]), the expected result is 7
When max_coins(nums=[2,2,2]), the expected result is 12

hints: Triple loop over length, l, k
